requirements.PPRF-CORE-001: The participant profile is a single module in src/participant-profile/ containing no renderer-specific logic; both mounts render the same header, spaghetti and measure-table composition from the same cleaned-row input.
requirements.PPRF-CORE-002: SafetyViz.participantProfile(el, data, config) renders the profile standalone, ingesting the standard long-lab data contract.
requirements.PPRF-CORE-003: When docked, the profile renders into an sv-profile shell slot below the host chart card and is fed the host chart's cleaned rows, performing no second data ingest.
requirements.PPRF-CORE-004: The standalone mount renders the house shell chrome — sidebar controls with the chart card hidden, the profile block owning the main column — and shows an idle note naming the listen target until a selection arrives.
requirements.PPRF-CORE-005: profileDock(container, settings) mounts the dock imperatively: it consumes the host's pre-cleaned rows verbatim — no checkInputs, no cleanData — installs no event listener, and is driven via show/clear/resize/destroy.
requirements.PPRF-HDR-001: The profile header shows the participant id, the demographic fields configured in config.details, the computed R Ratio, and P_ALT where computable.
requirements.PPRF-HDR-002: The header provides a Clear affordance that empties the profile and, when docked, clears the host chart's selection highlight.
requirements.PPRF-HDR-003: When config.participantProfileURL is set, the header renders a link-out whose href substitutes the participant id for the {id} template token; no link renders when the setting is absent.
requirements.PPRF-SPAG-001: The labs-over-time spaghetti draws one day-indexed line per key measure, toggling between ×ULN and ×baseline display, with non-key measures available behind a toggle.
requirements.PPRF-SPAG-002: Each spaghetti measure applies its per-measure reference cut: the cut line appears on hover/focus and points at or above the cut render filled while points below render unfilled.
requirements.PPRF-SPAG-003: Each measure receives a stable categorical palette color shared by its spaghetti line, sparkline and inset, cycling when a profile carries more measures than colors.
requirements.PPRF-TBL-001: The measure table lists every measure — key measures first, extras behind a "show N additional" toggle — with N, min, median, max and an inline sparkline per row.
requirements.PPRF-TBL-002: Activating a row's sparkline expands an inset line chart showing the LLN–ULN normal-range band, population-extent guides defaulting to the 1st/99th percentiles, and marked outliers.
requirements.PPRF-TBL-003: The inset line chart computes a padded y-domain unioning the participant's values, the population extent and the finite normal-range limits, and paints the LLN–ULN band and dashed population-extent guides in chart space with outlier points filled.
requirements.PPRF-TBL-004: The extras toggle uses the original's "Show N additional measure(s):" copy, reveals and re-hides the non-key measure rows, and collapses any open extra-row inset when extras are hidden.
requirements.PPRF-TBL-005: The optional participant record listing (listing: true) renders the participant's rows through the shared listing renderer with columns derived from the lab mapping by default, a listing_cols override, and paging.
requirements.PPRF-STEP-001: When the selection holds more than one participant, the dock collapses to a stepper (◀ k of N · id ▶) that renders the full profile for the current participant and keeps the host chart highlight in sync while stepping.
requirements.PPRF-STEP-002: The stepper orders participants worst quadrant first, falling back to peak severity where quadrant classification does not apply.
requirements.PPRF-EVT-001: Docked, the module derives its content from the host chart's selection state including clear; standalone, it listens for participantsSelected (payload {detail:{data: ids}}) on a configurable target; in neither mount does it dispatch selection events itself.
requirements.PPRF-EVT-002: setSelected() takes the same path as the participantsSelected listener, and outbound coordination is callbacks only: stepper navigation reports through on_step and Clear through on_clear — the module dispatches no selection events.
requirements.PPRF-HEP-001: hep-explorer enables the dock by default in both views: scatter click keeps the on-chart visit trace and rugs while the module replaces the bespoke drawDetail, which is deleted.
requirements.PPRF-HEP-002: In the composite view, focusing a single participant (click or selector) opens the docked profile and a multi-participant selection shows the stepper.
requirements.PPRF-HEP-003: In the composite view, focusing a single participant — point click or the shared Participants selector — opens the full docked profile, not the stepper.
requirements.PPRF-HEP-004: The migration ribbon hand-off arrives in the composite view with the dock opened on the carried cohort, with no dock-specific edits to the migration, composite or selection code paths.
requirements.PPRF-HEP-005: A background click (empty selection dispatch) clears the selection, empties the dock slot, and the shell's :empty rule hides the block.
requirements.PPRF-OE-001: outlier-explorer enables the dock by default (config-on): the shared participant profile mounts in the shell profile slot with the host lab mappings, optional measure_values map, and profile_details passed through, and its feed rows are derived ONCE per data load through the shared hep-core cleaners — never per gesture.
requirements.PPRF-OE-002: Clicking a point feeds the docked profile the full participant story via the participantsSelected dispatch on the shell root (SOE-API-003), SUPPLEMENTING the linked listing, which stays; the dock Clear affordance routes through the host clear path and empties both.
requirements.PPRF-OE-003: A background click empties the dock, and any control-driven redraw resets the selection and the dock in the same render preamble — the dock and the chart never disagree about what is selected.
requirements.PPRF-DD-001: delta-delta dispatches the house participantsSelected event (payload {detail:{data: ids}}, bubbling) on the shell root for point clicks AND clears — closing the renderer’s missing-dispatch gap (sv#88 SELN-4) as the dock prerequisite; the dispatch fires whether or not the dock is enabled.
requirements.PPRF-DD-002: Clicking a delta-delta point opens the docked FULL profile: the underlying long-lab rows are cleaned once per data load (the baseline-vs-comparison delta is not re-encoded — the profile shows the full series), the point is border-highlighted, and the header details default to the host details minus the participant id.
requirements.PPRF-DD-003: delta-delta gains a clear gesture: an empty-canvas click restores the point borders, resets the annotation, and dispatches the empty selection so the dock empties; the dock Clear affordance and every control-driven redraw take the same clear path.
requirements.PPRF-DD-004: The bespoke per-measure detail table (src/delta-delta/listing.js) is deleted in the adopting change: the docked profile supersedes it as the sole detail view, and the superseded matrix rows (SDD-FUNC-006 table portion, SDD-REG-011/014/016..025) are marked superseded in safety-delta-delta.md.
requirements.PPRF-SSP-001: A multi-participant brush collapses the docked profile to the worst-first cohort stepper ("1 of N", module ranking), and stepper navigation border-emphasizes the stepped point on the chart transiently — without touching the brush selection and without re-dispatching.
requirements.PPRF-SSP-002: shift-plot enables the dock by default (config-on): a single-point brush shows the FULL docked profile (no stepper) beside the retained linked listing, with feed rows derived ONCE per data load from the retained rawData — never from the pair-per-participant chartPairs — through the shared hep-core cleaners, and visitn_col mapped from the host's visit_order_col.
requirements.PPRF-SSP-003: Clearing the selection — the tiny-click path, the dock Clear affordance (routed through the host clear path), or any control-driven re-render — empties the dock and restores the uniform point emphasis, with the empty dispatch travelling the same participantsSelected pipe.
requirements.PPRF-SSP-004: The participantsSelected dispatch target moves from the host element to the shell root so the docked feed hears every dispatch (SSP-API-003 alignment with the house contract); the event still bubbles, so element-level listeners — the original SSP-API-003 surface — keep working.
requirements.PPRF-SH-001: histogram enables the dock by default (config-on): the shared participant profile mounts in the shell profile slot with the host lab mappings, optional measure_values map, and profile_details passed through, and its feed rows are derived ONCE per data load through the shared hep-core cleaners — never per gesture.
requirements.PPRF-SH-002: Clicking (or keyboard-activating) a row of the shared linked listing focuses that participant — a NEW host selection state (state.selectedId) dispatches the house participantsSelected event on the shell root, opening the docked FULL profile while the listing stays with the participant's rows highlighted; the row affordance is presence-of-callback opt-in (onListingRowClick), leaving the other shared-listing consumers byte-identical.
requirements.PPRF-SH-003: Clearing the focus — the dock Clear affordance (routed through the host clear path, retaining the listing), a NEW bin click (which replaces the listing), or any control-driven re-render — empties the dock, un-highlights the rows, and dispatches the empty selection.
requirements.PPRF-QT-001: qt-explorer enables the dock by default (config-on) and gains a minimal outlier-scatter point-click selection (state.selectedId, single-select only): a point click emphasizes the point and dispatches the house participantsSelected event on the shell root, opening the docked FULL profile; no listing exists and none is added — the dock is the sole drill-down surface.
requirements.PPRF-QT-002: The profile's long-lab contract is mapped for interval (ECG) measures: feed rows synthesize a unit ULN (__qt_profile_uln = 1) so the spaghetti renders OBSERVED milliseconds and nothing drops on the ULN guard; the identity measure_values map over the host's measures makes the ECG parameters the KEY measures; per-QTc cuts carry the first absolute threshold (450 ms default) with a NaN defaults entry leaving Heart Rate cut-free; the host's BASE value column is NOT passed as the profile's baseline flag.
requirements.PPRF-QT-003: An empty-canvas click clears the selection and empties the dock, and every render — including a view switch — resets both in the render preamble; on the central/categorical views (no participant marks) the dock idles, still mounted.
requirements.PPRF-QT-004: The gesture is single-select only, but the dock honors the full contract for externally-fed cohorts: a root-level multi-id dispatch opens the worst-first stepper, stepping emphasizes the stepped point without re-dispatching, and dock Clear empties the cohort with the empty dispatch travelling the same pipe.
requirements.PPRF-COV-001: The non-lab renderers ae-explorer and ae-timelines carry a one-line coverage-doc note deferring their profile adoption to the AE-domain v2 sections — they ingest adverse-event records, not the measure-per-visit long-lab contract the dock consumes.
requirements.PPRF-ACC-001: The profile is keyboard-operable throughout — stepper arrows, sparkline expansion and Clear — with labeled regions, :focus-visible rings, and reduced-motion-safe transitions.
requirements.PPRF-GATE-001: The standalone renderer ships the full done-gate: gallery card, live linked-charts demo, guide, evidence page, API reference and schema, with evidence.json refreshed and canonical Linux baselines regenerated.
